summary: Soundboard recording of a stellar 1972 show with intense Stella Blue, smoking Other One and crisp mix despite minor drum‑mix quirks.
review: The reviewer rates the performance a perfect five stars, praising a mellow first set that builds into an electrifying second set highlighted by a blistering Loser, China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider, Playing In The Band and a 22‑minute Other One that “crater[s]” with spacey jams. The soundboard source is described as a significant upgrade with clear instrument separation, though the drum kit sits slightly back in the mix and there’s an occasional cut.

ai_rating.rationale: Exceptional musical performance (standout songs, strong flow) paired with a good‑to‑excellent soundboard recording; only one detailed review limits confidence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp overall mix and clear instrument separation; drum kit slightly recessed, a brief cut noted but no major issues.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: Mellow, vocal‑rich first set transitions into a high‑energy second set with tight segues (e.g., WRS prelude → Me & My Uncle) and a powerful jam sequence culminating in a 22‑minute Other One.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar tone is praised throughout; his vocals on Jack Straw are called an instant GD standard and he “lets Stella shine”.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s backing vocals are highlighted on Mexicali Blues, I Know You Rider, and Jack Straw, adding an important X‑factor.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir contributes early ballads and a surprising prelude into Me & My Uncle; his vocal work is noted as part of the set’s mellow vibe.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s “tinkering and exploring” on keyboards receives positive mention.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill’s drumming blends well with Phil’s bass journey, though the drum kit sits back in the mix and isn’t overly prominent.
